title: VN — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations

sections.title: CFJ (industry-level, L7)
sections.content:
> **Transfer cargo and passengers between vessel and landside transport networks safely, lawfully, and on schedule** — from vessel arrival in the approach channel to handover onto road, rail, or inland waterway.

Mechanism-free. Applies to container, bulk, RoRo, liquid, cruise, ferry and inland-port operators alike.

---

sections.title: VN hierarchy (L7 → L6 → L5 → L4)
sections.content:
### L7 Maritime Cargo & Passenger Movement Ecosystem

### L6 Core process steps (sequential)

| L6 | Name | Job family (verb + object) | Activates for |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Vessel Arrival & Berthing | Guide vessel from fairway to berth and secure alongside | All vessel classes |
| L6b | Vessel–Shore Interface | Bridge vessel and quay for power, cargo, and passenger transfer | Container, bulk, RoRo, cruise, ferry |
| L6c | Terminal Cargo Handling | Move cargo across quay wall between ship and yard | Container, bulk, RoRo, liquid |
| L6d | Yard & Storage Operations | Store, stack, and track cargo units on terminal | Container, bulk, liquid |
| L6e | Landside Interchange | Hand cargo off to road, rail, or inland waterway | All cargo classes |
| L6f | Passenger & Vehicle Embarkation | Embark/disembark passengers and vehicles | Cruise, ferry, RoRo-pax |

### L6 Horizontal (cross-cutting)

| L6H | Name | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| L6H-ENE | **Port Energy & Power Infrastructure** | Supply, store, and dispatch electrical power to all terminal processes · **Mein Solar L6 segment** |
| L6H-SAF | Port Security & Safety | ISPS, port police, fire, dangerous-goods management |
| L6H-MAINT | Maintenance & Civil Engineering | Keep quay walls, pavement, cranes, fleet operational |
| L6H-IT | Terminal Operating Systems & Port IT | Gate, TOS, PCS, single-window customs |
| L6H-ENV | Environmental Compliance & Emissions | Air, water, noise monitoring; EU ETS/FuelEU reporting |

### L5 equipment within L6H-ENE (Mein Solar's home segment — **all sibling alternatives**)

| L5 | Description | Typical size | Incumbents |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shore Power / OPS Systems | Cold-ironing connection allowing berthed ships to shut down auxiliary engines | 1–20 MVA per berth; 60/50 Hz conversion | Siemens, ABB, Cavotec, Schneider, Wärtsilä |
| **Large-Scale BESS & Port Microgrid** | Containerised Li-ion BESS buffering peak loads, OPS demand, and renewables · **← Mein Solar PRIMARY POSITION** | 1–25 MWh | ABB, Wärtsilä, Fluence, Sungrow, Tesla |
| Port-Side Solar PV Generation | Rooftop/canopy PV on warehouses, MSCs, office buildings · **← Mein Solar SECONDARY POSITION** | 0.5–10 MWp | BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck, local EPCs |
| MV Grid Connection & Substation | 10/20/110 kV connection to DSO/TSO, transformers, switchgear | 10–150 MVA | Siemens, ABB, Schneider, Eaton, Hitachi |
| High-Power Charging Infrastructure | DC fast charging for reach stackers, terminal tractors, ferries, shore-vehicles · **← Mein Solar SECONDARY POSITION** | 150 kW–3 MW per dispenser | ABB E-mobility, Kempower, Alpitronic, ads-tec, Kostad |
| Diesel Standby Generation (legacy) | Backup gensets for black-start and safety loads | 0.5–5 MVA | Caterpillar, Cummins, MTU |

### L4 subsystems per L5

#### L5 Shore Power / OPS Systems
| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Frequency Converter | 50↔60 Hz static converter rated to vessel demand |
| HV Cable Management System | Motorised reel/crane for ship connection |
| Berth-side Connection Box | Plug, interlock, protection relay per IEC 80005-1 |

#### L5 Large-Scale BESS & Port Microgrid *(Mein Solar primary)*
| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Battery Container (LFP, BMS, HVAC, fire suppression) | 1–5 MWh containerised stack, IP54, NOVEC 1230 |
| Bidirectional Power Conversion System (PCS) | 4-quadrant inverter, grid-forming capable for microgrid mode |
| Energy Management System (EMS) | Dispatch logic: peak shaving, OPS support, PV firming, market bidding |
| MV Transformer & Switchgear | Step-up to 10/20 kV, SF6-free RMU, protection |
| Civil Works & Fire Clearance | Foundation, bund, fence, Feuerwehr access per VdS |

#### L5 Port-Side Solar PV Generation *(Mein Solar secondary)*
| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| PV Modules & Mounting | Mono c-Si modules on warehouse roof or canopy |
| String/Central Inverter | DC→AC conversion with Q(U) grid-support |
| DC Combiner & Monitoring | String-level metering, arc-fault, isolation |

#### L5 MV Grid Connection & Substation
| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| MV Transformer (oil/dry) | Step-up/down to DSO feeder voltage |
| MV Switchgear (RMU, GIS) | Protection, isolation, remote trip |
| Protection Relays & Metering | IEC 61850 protection, revenue metering |

#### L5 High-Power Charging Infrastructure *(Mein Solar secondary)*
| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| DC Charger (CCS2/MCS) | 150 kW–3 MW output, liquid-cooled cable |
| Charging Management System | Dispenser scheduling, auth, grid-aware dispatch |
| Vehicle-Side Connector & Safety Interlock | Plug, earth-check, insulation monitor |

#### L5 Diesel Standby Generation (legacy)
| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Diesel Engine-Generator Set | Prime mover + alternator |
| Fuel Tank & Bund | Day tank, bulk storage, spill containment |
| Auto-Transfer Switch & Control | Grid-loss detection, automatic start/transfer |

**Total units: ~42** (1 L7 · 6 L6 core · 5 L6H · 6 L5 · ~24 L4). Inside 20–60 target.

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Strategic Adjacency Analyses
sections.content:
### 04e — Direct Competence Transfer Scan

**Scope:** entire port VN. Only LOW/MEDIUM effort units listed.

| VN Unit | Level | Competence Required | Mein Solar Has It? | Adaptation Effort | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Large-Scale BESS & Port Microgrid | L5 | Containerised Li-ion EPC, PCS, MV, EMS, VDE-AR-N 4110 | YES | LOW | Direct product fit; only incremental IP upgrade for salt-air corrosion (IP55→66, stainless fasteners) |
| Port-Side Solar PV Generation | L5 | Rooftop PV EPC, DSO grid interface | YES | LOW | Mein Solar's incumbent PV business; same grid code |
| HPC Charging Infrastructure | L5 | MV coupling, BESS-buffered HPC, site civil | PARTIAL | LOW | Feature #7 in product decomp covers BESS-buffered HPC; needs partnership with charger OEM (ABB, Kempower, Alpitronic) |
| MV Grid Connection & Substation | L5 | 10/20 kV transformer, switchgear, protection, DSO acceptance | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Mein Solar delivers MV scope as part of BESS EPC; as standalone would need to bid against T1 OEMs |
| Shore Power / OPS Systems | L5 | 50↔60 Hz frequency conversion, HV ship-connection hardware, IEC 80005-1 | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | MV + civil + EMS scope transfers; frequency converter and ship-interface hardware (Cavotec reel, IEC 80005) are new |
| Environmental Compliance & Emissions (L6H) | L6H | Monitoring EMS extension, renewable attribution | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | EMS can attribute clean electrons to OPS draw; sales-adjacent to BESS scope |

Excluded (HIGH effort, not listed in detail): Quay cranes, vessel pilotage, stevedoring, TOS software, port security, dredging.

### 04f — Strategic Adjacency Expansion (within L6H-ENE)

Current segment: **L6H-ENE Port Energy & Power Infrastructure**
Current position: **L5 Large-Scale BESS & Port Microgrid**

| Adjacent L5 Unit | Current Incumbents | Competence Gap | Investment to Bridge | Strategic Fit (1-10) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Port-Side Solar PV Generation | BayWa r.e., Enerparc, Goldbeck | None — incumbent business | <3 mo, <€100k sales effort | **9** | >90% overlap; simply bundle PV + BESS + MV + OPS as one EPC scope |
| HPC Charging Infrastructure | ABB E-mobility, Kempower, Alpitronic, ads-tec, Kostad | DC charger hardware, OCPP backend, vehicle certification | 6–12 mo, €0.5–1M (channel partner, not build) | **8** | ~60% overlap; Mein Solar owns site/MV/BESS, partners with charger OEM. Directly activates feature #7 of product. |
| MV Grid Connection & Substation | Siemens, ABB, Schneider, Hitachi | Standalone utility-scale transformer/switchgear project delivery without the BESS wrapper | 12–18 mo, €1–2M | **5** | ~40% overlap; winnable only as part of bundled scope, not standalone vs. T1 OEMs |
| Shore Power / OPS Systems | Siemens, ABB, Cavotec, Schneider, Wärtsilä | 50↔60 Hz frequency converter, IEC 80005-1 ship-connection hardware, vessel-side interface expertise | 18–24 mo, €2–4M (freq-converter partnership or license) | **6** | ~35% overlap; MV/civil/EMS transfers, but converter + ship interface is a new product line. Strong only if bundled with BESS-for-OPS-firming play |

Excluded (<30% overlap): Diesel Standby Generation (wrong fuel/tech; legacy declining).

### 04g — Vertical Move-Up Assessment

| Current Position | Target (1 level up) | What Must Be Added | Competitors at Target | Feasibility | Economic Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L5 Large-Scale BESS & Port Microgrid | **L6H-ENE Port Energy & Power Infrastructure integrator** | Own full energy scope: OPS + BESS + PV + MV + HPC + EMS + O&M as one port-wide service contract | Siemens Smart Infrastructure, ABB, Wärtsilä Energy, Schneider Electric | **MEDIUM** | Mein Solar already has ~55% of the L6H-ENE scope (BESS + PV + MV + HPC-ready + EMS). Adding OPS + unified port-EMS = €2–4M R&D + 1 frequency-converter partnership. Port authority TAM (Hamburg, BLG, Duisport, NPorts) is ~€500M–1B across DE ports 2026–2032, vs. individual BESS TAM ~€50–100M. **Move-up is economically rational if Mein Solar wins 1–2 reference ports and extends into consortium bids.** Blocker is track-record credibility in public port procurement — no reference yet. |

---

sections.title: Completeness checklist (Phase 2 gates)
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| CFJ industry-level (no product/tech reference) | ✅ |
| No orphan nodes (every unit has parent) | ✅ |
| Valid level transitions (L7→L6→L5→L4) | ✅ |
| ≥3 L4 children per L5 | ✅ (6 L5 × 3+ L4 each) |
| No solution-specific L7/L6 names | ✅ |
| Three-question naming test | ✅ (domain-standard terms throughout) |
| Unit count 15–60 | ✅ (~42) |
| Mein Solar position marked | ✅ (PRIMARY + 2 SECONDARY) |

---

sections.title: Key market context (supports Phase 3 scoring)
sections.content:
| Fact | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| EU shore-power mandate | TEN-T core ports must offer OPS for container + cruise >5000 GT by **2030** | AFIR Reg. 2023/1804 |
| Typical OPS demand | Cruise ship up to **16 MW**, container ship **~7.5 MW** | Hamburg Port Authority |
| Hamburg deployment | **10 container + 4 cruise** OPS connections by 2025 | portofhamburg.com |
| German BESS market | **842 MW added 2025**, 41 GW projected by 2037 | Modo Energy / BNetzA |
| Port BESS reference | ABB Pier 300 Port of LA — microgrid + flywheel storage | ABB |
| Electric reach stacker | SANY 50t with **512 kWh** pack; Kalmar 45t electric | Kalmar / SANY |
| Environment constraint | Salt-air coastal → IP55+ / stainless hardware required on standard BESS container | VDE / IEC 60721-3 |

---

```json
    {"title": "Energy-Storage.News — 2026 German BESS regulatory agenda", "url": "https://www.energy-storage.news/moment-of-truth-the-2026-regulatory-agenda-for-large-battery-storage-in-germany/"},
    {"title": "ScienceDirect — Coordinated energy and logistics scheduling for electrified seaports", "url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0306261925013753"}
  ]
}
```

rows.Description: 10/20/110 kV connection to DSO/TSO, transformers, switchgear
DC fast charging for reach stackers, terminal tractors, ferries, shore-vehicles · **← Mein Solar SECONDARY POSITION**

rows.Competence Required: Containerised Li-ion EPC, PCS, MV, EMS, VDE-AR-N 4110
rows.Rationale: Direct product fit; only incremental IP upgrade for salt-air corrosion (IP55→66, stainless fasteners)

rows.Competence Required: Rooftop PV EPC, DSO grid interface
rows.Rationale: Mein Solar's incumbent PV business; same grid code

rows.Competence Required: MV coupling, BESS-buffered HPC, site civil
rows.Rationale: Feature #7 in product decomp covers BESS-buffered HPC; needs partnership with charger OEM (ABB, Kempower, Alpitronic)

rows.Competence Required: 10/20 kV transformer, switchgear, protection, DSO acceptance
rows.Rationale: Mein Solar delivers MV scope as part of BESS EPC; as standalone would need to bid against T1 OEMs

rows.Competence Required: 50↔60 Hz frequency conversion, HV ship-connection hardware, IEC 80005-1
rows.Rationale: MV + civil + EMS scope transfers; frequency converter and ship-interface hardware (Cavotec reel, IEC 80005) are new
EMS can attribute clean electrons to OPS draw; sales-adjacent to BESS scope
>90% overlap; simply bundle PV + BESS + MV + OPS as one EPC scope

rows.Current Incumbents: ABB E-mobility, Kempower, Alpitronic, ads-tec, Kostad
rows.Competence Gap: DC charger hardware, OCPP backend, vehicle certification
rows.Rationale: ~60% overlap; Mein Solar owns site/MV/BESS, partners with charger OEM. Directly activates feature #7 of product.

rows.Competence Gap: Standalone utility-scale transformer/switchgear project delivery without the BESS wrapper
rows.Rationale: ~40% overlap; winnable only as part of bundled scope, not standalone vs. T1 OEMs

rows.Competence Gap: 50↔60 Hz frequency converter, IEC 80005-1 ship-connection hardware, vessel-side interface expertise
rows.Rationale: ~35% overlap; MV/civil/EMS transfers, but converter + ship interface is a new product line. Strong only if bundled with BESS-for-OPS-firming play

rows.What Must Be Added: Own full energy scope: OPS + BESS + PV + MV + HPC + EMS + O&M as one port-wide service contract
rows.Competitors at Target: Siemens Smart Infrastructure, ABB, Wärtsilä Energy, Schneider Electric
rows.Economic Rationale: Mein Solar already has ~55% of the L6H-ENE scope (BESS + PV + MV + HPC-ready + EMS). Adding OPS + unified port-EMS = €2–4M R&D + 1 frequency-converter partnership. Port authority TAM (Hamburg, BLG, Duisport, NPorts) is ~€500M–1B across DE ports 2026–2032, vs. individual BESS TAM ~€50–100M. **Move-up is economically rational if Mein Solar wins 1–2 reference ports and extends into consortium bids.** Blocker is track-record credibility in public port procurement — no reference yet.

rows.Check: No orphan nodes (every unit has parent)

rows.Value: TEN-T core ports must offer OPS for container + cruise >5000 GT by **2030**
Cruise ship up to **16 MW**, container ship **~7.5 MW**
ABB Pier 300 Port of LA — microgrid + flywheel storage
SANY 50t with **512 kWh** pack; Kalmar 45t electric
Salt-air coastal → IP55+ / stainless hardware required on standard BESS container

vertical_move_up.economic_rationale: DE port-energy TAM 2026–2032 ≈ €500M–1B across Hamburg/BLG/Duisport/NPorts; single-BESS TAM ≈ €50–100M. Move-up rational IF Mein Solar wins 1–2 reference ports and extends via consortium bids. Blocker: zero public-port procurement references today.
vertical_move_up.acquisition_alternative_flag: If organic build stalls, acquiring a mid-size port-electrification integrator (Cavotec subsidiary, specialist Nordic OPS firm) is the faster path.
